mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales reflects strengthening consumer demand that drives corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month change is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4 percent indicates an overheated labor market that generates wage-push inflation and Federal Reserve hawkishness, depressing S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or average return is not negative (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation jointly raise real discount rates and compress equity cash flow valuations more than either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production growth in late-cycle expansions signals impending earnings compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, declines in INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's information coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in consumer price inflation reduces nominal uncertainty and supports real consumer spending power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Months with declining CPIAUCSL are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand that immediately pressures corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative monthly RSAFS changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity that boosts corporate operating leverage and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth in the top quartile are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and accelerating payroll employment growth creates a wage-price spiral that prompts tighter Federal Reserve policy, jointly depressing equity valuations more than either variable alone.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 300 thousand, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term is insignificant in regression (p > 0.05).
